content.problemStatement:
Chef is a very lazy person. Whatever work is supposed to be finished in xxx units of time, he finishes it in m∗xm * xm∗x units of time. But there is always a limit to laziness, so he delays the work by at max ddd units of time. Given x,m,dx, m, dx,m,d, find the maximum time taken by Chef to complete the work.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains a single line of input, three integers x,m,dx, m, dx,m,d.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line answer to the problem.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers x, m and d.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum time Chef can take, which is min(x*m, x+d).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case we compute min(x*m, x+d):
1) min(1*1, 1+0) = min(1,1) = 1
2) min(1*3, 1+1) = min(3,2) = 2
3) min(2*2, 2+3) = min(4,5) = 4

hidden.label: general case where delay limits time
general case where work time is smaller

solution.approach: For each test case compute the time Chef would take without any limit, which is x*m. The laziness limit allows him to delay at most d units, so the latest finishing time cannot exceed x+d. Therefore the answer is the smaller of the two values: ans = min(x*m, x+d). This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
